title: Publisher Buffers, Drain, Admission, and Bounded Batching · Type-System Trace: Blocked Unblocked Flow
summary: A publisher awaits a boolean from publish, ignores false, buffers unbounded work during broker flow control, and batches until memory exhaustion while upstream requests continue to receive success. Planned artifact: Add and verify the blocked unblocked flow increment in publisher admission and buffer-pressure controller. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Respond to blocked and unblocked connection events, broker flow control, alarms, and local drain evidence without confusing their scopes.
Batch by count, bytes, age, confirm window, and cancellation while retaining each message result and fairness.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Predict Blocked Unblocked Flow · Theory Blocked Unblocked Flow
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory messaging team handles case 1086 in a quorum queue during a planned rolling maintenance window. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to propagate blocked, byte capacity, publish boolean, and drain state into upstream admission; show recovery after the changed failure is removed; then lose a quorum member and recompute majority availability. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.
